briefdescription.para: VDMA data transfer direction.
DMA transfer from memory.
Frame Sync Source Selection.
GenLock Source Selection.
GenLock Mode Constants.
Interrupt type for setting up callback.
Error Interrupt Type.
Flag to signal that device is ready to be used.
Debug Configuration Parameter Constants (C_ENABLE_DEBUG_INFO_*)
Callback type for general interrupts.

parameterdescription.para: is a callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the callback functions, and passed back to the upper layer when the callback is called.
indicates the detailed type(s) of the interrupt. Its value equals 'OR'ing one or more XAXIVDMA_IXR_* values defined in xaxivdma_hw.h

briefdescription.para: Callback type for Error interrupt.

parameterdescription.para: is a callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the callback function, and it is passed back to the upper layer when the callback is called.
is a bit mask indicating the cause of the error. Its value equals 'OR'ing one or more XAXIVDMA_IXR_* values defined in xaxivdma_hw.h

briefdescription.para: Looks up the device configuration based on the unique device ID/BaseAddress.

detaileddescription.para: A table contains the configuration info for each device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration table entry corresponding to the given device ID/BaseAddress, or NULL if no match is found.
In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

briefdescription.para: Initialize the driver with hardware configuration.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the pointer to the hardware configuration structure
is the virtual address map for the device

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if everything goes fine
XST_FAILURE if reset the hardware failed, need system reset to recover

simplesect.para: If channel fails reset, then it will be set as invalid

briefdescription.para: This function resets one DMA channel.

detaileddescription.para: The registers will be default values after the reset

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the channel to work on, use XAXIVDMA_READ/WRITE

simplesect.para: None
Due to undeterminism of system delays, check the reset status through XAxiVdma_ResetNotDone(). If direction is invalid, do nothing.

briefdescription.para: This function checks one DMA channel for reset completion.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the channel to work on, use XAXIVDMA_READ/WRITE

listitem.para: 0 if reset is done
1 if reset is ongoing

simplesect.para: We do not check for channel validity, because channel is marked as invalid before reset is done

briefdescription.para: Check whether a DMA channel is busy.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the channel to work on, use XAXIVDMA_READ/WRITE

listitem.para: Non-zero if the channel is busy
Zero if the channel is idle

briefdescription.para: Get the current frame that hardware is working on.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the channel to work on, use XAXIVDMA_READ/WRITE

simplesect.para: The current frame that the hardware is working on
If returned frame number is out of range, then the channel is invalid

briefdescription.para: Get the version of the hardware.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on

simplesect.para: The version of the hardware

briefdescription.para: Get the status of a channel.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the channel to work on, use XAXIVDMA_READ/WRITE

simplesect.para: The status of the channel
An invalid return value indicates that channel is invalid

briefdescription.para: Configure Line Buffer Threshold.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the value to set threshold
is the DMA channel to work on

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful
XST_FAILURE otherwise
XST_NO_FEATURE if access to Threshold register is disabled

briefdescription.para: Start parking mode on a certain frame.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the frame to park on
is the channel to work on, use XAXIVDMA_READ/WRITE

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if everything is fine
XST_INVALID_PARAM if . channel is invalid . FrameIndex is invalid . Direction is invalid

briefdescription.para: Exit parking mode, the channel will return to circular buffer mode.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the channel to work on, use XAXIVDMA_READ/WRITE

simplesect.para: None
If channel is invalid, then do nothing

briefdescription.para: Start frame count enable on one channel.

detaileddescription.para: This is needed to start limiting the number of frames to transfer so that software can check the data etc after hardware stops transfer.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the channel to work on, use XAXIVDMA_READ/WRITE

simplesect.para: None

briefdescription.para: Enable specific interrupts for a channel.

detaileddescription.para: Interrupts not specified by the mask will not be affected

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the DMA channel, use XAXIVDMA_READ or XAXIVDMA_WRITE
is the bit mask for the interrupts to be enabled

simplesect.para: None
If channel is invalid, then nothing is done

briefdescription.para: Disable specific interrupts for a channel.

detaileddescription.para: Interrupts not specified by the mask will not be affected

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the bit mask for the interrupts to be disabled
is the DMA channel, use XAXIVDMA_READ or XAXIVDMA_WRITE

simplesect.para: None
If channel is invalid, then nothing is done

briefdescription.para: Get the pending interrupts of a channel.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the DMA channel, use XAXIVDMA_READ or XAXIVDMA_WRITE

simplesect.para: The bit mask for the currently pending interrupts
If Direction is invalid, return 0

briefdescription.para: Clear the pending interrupts specified by the bit mask.

detaileddescription.para: Interrupts not specified by the mask will not be affected

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the DMA channel, use XAXIVDMA_READ or XAXIVDMA_WRITE
is the bit mask for the interrupts to be cleared

simplesect.para: None
If channel is invalid, then nothing is done

memberdef.argsstring: (XAxiVdma *InstancePtr, u32 BdAddrPhys, u32 BdAddrVirt, int NumBds, u16 Direction)
memberdef.name: XAxiVdma_SetBdAddrs

briefdescription.para: Set BD addresses to be different.

detaileddescription.para: In some systems, it is convenient to put BDs into a certain region of the memory. This function enables that.

InstancePtr
is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
BdAddrPhys
is the physical starting address for BDs
BdAddrVirt
is the Virtual starting address for BDs. For systems that do not use MMU, then virtual address is the same as physical address
NumBds
is the number of BDs to setup with. This is required to be the same as the number of frame stores for that channel
Direction
is the channel direction
XST_SUCCESS for a successful setup
XST_DEVICE_BUSY if the DMA channel is not idle, BDs are still being used
XST_INVALID_PARAM if parameters not valid
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if the channel is invalid
We assume that the memory region starting from BdAddrPhys and BdAddrVirt are large enough to hold all the BDs.

briefdescription.para: Get a channel.

parameterdescription.para: is the DMA engine to work on
is the direction for the channel to get

simplesect.para: The pointer to the channel. Upon error, return NULL.
Since this function is internally used, we assume Direction is valid

briefdescription.para: Set the number of frame store buffers to use.

parameterdescription.para: is the XAxiVdma instance to operate on
is the number of frame store buffers to use.
is the channel to work on, use XAXIVDMA_READ/WRITE

simplesect.para:
- XST_SUCCESS if operation is successful
XST_FAILURE if operation fails.
XST_NO_FEATURE if access to FrameStore register is disabled
None

briefdescription.para: Get the number of frame store buffers to use.

parameterdescription.para: is the XAxiVdma instance to operate on
is the number of frame store buffers to use.
is the channel to work on, use XAXIVDMA_READ/WRITE

simplesect.para: None
None

briefdescription.para: Configure Frame Sync Source and valid only when C_USE_FSYNC is enabled.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the value to set the source of Frame Sync
is the DMA channel to work on

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful
XST_FAILURE if C_USE_FSYNC is disabled.

briefdescription.para: Configure Gen Lock Source.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the value to set the source of Gen Lock
is the DMA channel to work on

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful
XST_FAILURE if the channel is in GenLock Master Mode. if C_INCLUDE_INTERNAL_GENLOCK is disabled.

briefdescription.para: Check for DMA Channel Errors.

parameterdescription.para: is the XAxiVdma instance to operate on
is the channel to work on, use XAXIVDMA_READ/WRITE

simplesect.para:
- Errors seen on the channel
XST_INVALID_PARAM, when channel pointer is invalid.
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND, when the channel is not valid.
None

briefdescription.para: Clear DMA Channel Errors.

parameterdescription.para: is the XAxiVdma instance to operate on
is the channel to work on, use XAXIVDMA_READ/WRITE
is the mask of error bits to clear

simplesect.para:
- XST_SUCCESS, when error bits are cleared.
XST_INVALID_PARAM, when channel pointer is invalid.
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND, when the channel is not valid.
None

briefdescription.para: Masks the S2MM error interrupt for the provided error mask value.

parameterdescription.para: is the XAxiVdma instance to operate on
is the mask of error bits for which S2MM error interrupt can be disabled.
is the channel to work on, use XAXIVDMA_READ/WRITE

simplesect.para:
- XST_SUCCESS, when error bits are cleared.
XST_INVALID_PARAM, when channel pointer is invalid.
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND, when the channel is not valid.

The register S2MM_DMA_IRQ_MASK is only applicable from IPv6.01a which is added at offset XAXIVDMA_S2MM_DMA_IRQ_MASK_OFFSET. For older versions, this offset location is reserved and so the API does not have any effect.

briefdescription.para: Start a write operation.

detaileddescription.para: Write corresponds to send data from device to memory

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the pointer to the setup structure

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS for a successful submission
XST_DEVICE_BUSY if the DMA channel is not idle, BDs are still being used
XST_INVAID_PARAM if parameters in config structure not valid
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if the channel is invalid

briefdescription.para: Start a read operation.

detaileddescription.para: Read corresponds to send data from memory to device

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the pointer to the setup structure

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS for a successful submission
XST_DEVICE_BUSY if the DMA channel is not idle, BDs are still being used
XST_INVAID_PARAM if parameters in config structure not valid
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if the channel is invalid

briefdescription.para: Configure one DMA channel using the configuration structure.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the DMA channel to work on
is the pointer to the setup structure

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful
XST_DEVICE_BUSY if the DMA channel is not idle, BDs are still being used
XST_INVAID_PARAM if buffer address not valid, for example, unaligned address with no DRE built in the hardware, or Direction invalid
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if the channel is invalid

briefdescription.para: Configure buffer addresses for one DMA channel.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the DMA channel to work on
is the set of addresses for the transfers

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful
XST_DEVICE_BUSY if the DMA channel is not idle, BDs are still being used
XST_INVAID_PARAM if buffer address not valid, for example, unaligned address with no DRE built in the hardware, or Direction invalid
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if the channel is invalid

briefdescription.para: Start one DMA channel.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the DMA channel to work on

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if channel started successfully
XST_FAILURE otherwise
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if the channel is invalid
XST_INVALID_PARAM if Direction invalid

briefdescription.para: Stop one DMA channel.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the DMA channel to work on

simplesect.para: None
If channel is invalid, then do nothing on that channel

briefdescription.para: Dump registers of one DMA channel.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the DMA channel to work on

simplesect.para: None
If channel is invalid, then do nothing on that channel

briefdescription.para: Set the frame counter and delay counter for both channels.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the pointer to the configuration structure

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if setup finishes successfully
XST_INVALID_PARAM if the configuration structure has invalid values
Others if setting channel frame counter fails

simplesect.para: If channel is invalid, then do nothing on that channel

briefdescription.para: Get the frame counter and delay counter for both channels.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the configuration structure to contain return values

simplesect.para: None
If returned frame counter value is 0, then the channel is not valid

briefdescription.para: Interrupt handler for the read channel.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on

simplesect.para: None
If the channel is invalid, then no interrupt handling

briefdescription.para: Interrupt handler for the write channel.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on

simplesect.para: None
If the channel is invalid, then no interrupt handling

memberdef.argsstring: (XAxiVdma *InstancePtr, u32 HandlerType, void *CallBackFunc, void *CallBackRef, u16 Direction)
memberdef.name: XAxiVdma_SetCallBack

briefdescription.para: Set call back function and call back reference pointer for one channel.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the DMA engine to work on
is the interrupt type that this callback handles
is the call back function pointer
is the call back reference pointer
is the DMA channel, use XAXIVDMA_READ or XAXIVDMA_WRITE

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if everything is fine
XST_INVALID_PARAM if the handler type or direction invalid

simplesect.para: This function overwrites the existing interrupt handler and its reference pointer. The function sets the handlers even if the channels are invalid.

briefdescription.para: Runs a self-test on the driver/device.

detaileddescription.para: This test perform a reset of the VDMA device and checks the device is coming out of reset or not

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiVdma instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if self-test was successful
XST_FAILURE if the device is not coming out of reset.

simplesect.para: None.
